schema_name: IndoorClimbingGym Directory Data Schema with Web Enrichment Prompts

fields.name: name
fields.description: Official venue name as listed by the gym.
fields.desired_return: Exact branded gym name; do not add location descriptors unless part of the name.
fields.prompt:
Return the official gym name from the content. Use the exact branded wording as presented (e.g., 'Movement Baker', 'Bloc Shop Hochelaga'). Do not append the city or region unless it appears in the official name. If unclear, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Welcome to Movement Baker"
Output: Movement Baker

Content: "Bloc Shop Hochelaga is our east-end location"
Output: Bloc Shop Hochelaga

Now extract the official name:

fields.name: address
fields.description: Full formatted address as presented by the gym.
fields.desired_return: Single-line address including street number/name and any suite/unit; include city/region/postal/country if provided.
fields.prompt:
Return the full mailing address for '{{name}}' exactly as written in the content. Include street number, street name, suite/unit, city, region/state, postal code, and country when available. If multiple addresses appear, choose the one associated with '{{name}}'. If not found, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Address: 123 Climber Ln, Suite 200, Denver, CO 80205"
Output: 123 Climber Ln, Suite 200, Denver, CO 80205

Content: "Find us at 45 Wall St, Auckland 1010, New Zealand"
Output: 45 Wall St, Auckland 1010, New Zealand

Now extract the address for '{{name}}':

fields.name: city
fields.description: City or town where the gym is located.
fields.prompt:
Return the city or town for '{{name}}'. Do not include state/region or country. If not mentioned, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Located in Denver, Colorado"
Output: Denver

Content: "Our Toronto location"
Output: Toronto

Now extract the city for '{{name}}':

fields.name: region
fields.description: State, province, or primary administrative region.
fields.desired_return: State/province/region abbreviation or full name (no city/country).
fields.prompt:
Return the state/province/region for '{{name}}'. Use the abbreviation if given (e.g., CA, NSW) or full name if that's all that's provided. If not found, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Denver, CO 80205"
Output: CO

Content: "Vancouver, British Columbia"
Output: British Columbia

Now extract the region for '{{name}}':

fields.name: country
fields.prompt:
Return the country for '{{name}}'. If not mentioned, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "A premier gym in Toronto, Canada"
Output: Canada

Content: "Located in Paris, France"
Output: France

Now extract the country for '{{name}}':

fields.name: latitude
fields.prompt:
Return the latitude for '{{name}}' in decimal degrees if present in the content (maps/embed data). Use only numeric decimal format. If unavailable, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Coordinates: 37.7749, -122.4194"
Output: 37.7749

Content: "lat: -33.865143"
Output: -33.865143

Now extract the latitude for '{{name}}':

fields.name: longitude
fields.prompt:
Return the longitude for '{{name}}' in decimal degrees if present in the content (maps/embed data). Use only numeric decimal format. If unavailable, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Coordinates: 37.7749, -122.4194"
Output: -122.4194

Content: "lng: 151.209900"
Output: 151.2099

Now extract the longitude for '{{name}}':

fields.name: phone
fields.desired_return: Digits with optional leading '+', no spaces or hyphens.
fields.prompt:
Return the primary phone number for '{{name}}'. Normalize by removing spaces, dashes, and parentheses; keep a leading '+' if present. If multiple numbers exist, choose the main contact number. If none, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Call us at (303) 555-1212"
Output: 3035551212

Content: "+44 20 7946 0958"
Output: +442079460958

Now extract the phone for '{{name}}':

fields.name: email
fields.desired_return: Single email address in standard format (e.g., info@example.com).
fields.prompt:
Return the primary contact email for '{{name}}' if present. Use a single email address. If multiple are listed, choose the main/general contact. If none, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Email us: hello@movement.com"
Output: hello@movement.com

Content: "Contact: info@blocshop.com or teams@blocshop.com"
Output: info@blocshop.com

Now extract the email for '{{name}}':

fields.name: rating_count
fields.description: Number of Google Maps reviews for the gym.
fields.desired_return: Integer count of Google Maps reviews; no commas.
fields.prompt:
Return the total number of Google Maps reviews for '{{name}}' if present in the content (e.g., '4.6 (231 reviews)' or '231 Google reviews'). Output only the integer count without commas. If unavailable, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Google rating 4.6 (231 reviews)"
Output: 231

Content: "Rated 4.8 based on 1,245 Google reviews"
Output: 1245

Now extract the Google Maps review count for '{{name}}':

fields.name: website
fields.description: Primary website URL for the gym.
fields.prompt:
Return the main website URL for '{{name}}'. Prefer the official site over social profiles. Include protocol (https://). If not available, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Visit us at https://movementgyms.com/baker"
Output: https://movementgyms.com/baker

Content: "Website: http://www.blocshop.com"
Output: http://www.blocshop.com

Now extract the website for '{{name}}':

fields.name: hero_image
fields.description: Primary hero/cover image URL representing the gym.
fields.desired_return: Direct URL to a main hero or cover image (jpg/png/webp).
fields.prompt:
Return the URL of the best hero/cover image for '{{name}}' if present in the content (homepage hero, featured image, Open Graph image). Use a direct image URL (jpg/png/webp). If none, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "<meta property=\"og:image\" content=\"https://example.com/images/boulder-hero.jpg\" />"
Output: https://example.com/images/boulder-hero.jpg

Content: "Hero image: https://cdn.site.com/gyms/movement-baker.webp"
Output: https://cdn.site.com/gyms/movement-baker.webp

Now extract the hero image for '{{name}}':

fields.name: google_maps_url
fields.description: Google Maps place URL for the gym.
fields.desired_return: Full Google Maps place link (https://maps.google.com/... or https://goo.gl/maps/...).
fields.prompt:
Return the Google Maps URL for '{{name}}' if present in the content. Accept full Google Maps links including shortened goo.gl/maps. If multiple, choose the primary place link for '{{name}}'. If none, return 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Find us on Google Maps: https://goo.gl/maps/abcd1234"
Output: https://goo.gl/maps/abcd1234

Content: "Map: https://maps.google.com/?cid=123456789"
Output: https://maps.google.com/?cid=123456789

Now extract the Google Maps URL for '{{name}}':

fields.name: amenities
fields.prompt:
List confirmed amenities for '{{name}}' using ONLY these allowed values: showers|lockers|cafe|wifi|parking|sauna|kids_zone|pro_shop. Output pipe-delimited or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "We offer free wifi, lockers, and a cafe"
Output: wifi|lockers|cafe

Content: "Facilities include shower rooms and parking lot"
Output: showers|parking

Content: "Just climbing walls"
Output: null

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: custom_amenities
fields.description: Any additional amenities or facilities not in the standard amenities list.
fields.desired_return: Pipe-delimited list of any other amenities beyond the standard list (e.g., 'yoga_studio|massage|climbing_school').
fields.prompt:
List any additional amenities at '{{name}}' that are NOT in this list: showers, lockers, cafe, wifi, parking, sauna, kids_zone, pro_shop. Look for: climbing school, yoga classes, fitness area, gear shop, physical therapy, massage, coworking space, library, etc. Use underscores for spaces. Output pipe-delimited or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "We also have a yoga studio, climbing school, and physical therapy"
Output: yoga_studio|climbing_school|physical_therapy

Content: "Additional facilities: gear shop, library, coworking space"
Output: gear_shop|library|coworking_space

Content: "Just standard climbing facilities"
Output: null

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: climbing_types
fields.prompt:
Identify available disciplines for '{{name}}'. Allowed: bouldering|top_rope|lead|speed. Output pipe-delimited or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "We have bouldering walls and top-rope climbing"
Output: bouldering|top_rope

Content: "Featuring lead climbing, bouldering, and a speed wall"
Output: bouldering|lead|speed

Content: "Boulder-only gym"
Output: bouldering

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: route_reset_frequency
fields.prompt:
Determine reset cadence for '{{name}}'. Allowed: weekly|biweekly|monthly|quarterly|varies. If unknown, 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Routes reset every week" or reviews mention "weekly resets"
Output: weekly

Content: "We reset problems twice a month"
Output: biweekly

Content: "Route setting happens monthly"
Output: monthly

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: total_routes
fields.description: Approximate count of active boulder problems.
fields.prompt:
Return approximate number of boulder problems at '{{name}}'. Output integer or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Over 200 boulder problems"
Output: 200

Content: "150+ routes on the bouldering wall"
Output: 150

Content: "Rope climbing only, no bouldering"
Output: 0

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: wall_height_meters
fields.prompt:
Return max wall height for '{{name}}' in meters. If given in feet, convert to meters (feet × 0.3048). Output decimal or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Walls up to 50 feet"
Output: 15.24

Content: "40-foot high walls"
Output: 12.19

Content: "15 meter walls"
Output: 15.0

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: training_facilities
fields.prompt:
List training tools at '{{name}}'. Allowed: hangboard|campus_board|treadwall|weights|spray_wall. Pipe-delimited or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Training area with hangboards and campus boards"
Output: hangboard|campus_board

Content: "Fitness zone includes weights and a spray wall"
Output: weights|spray_wall

Content: "No dedicated training equipment"
Output: null

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: rental_equipment
fields.prompt:
List rental gear at '{{name}}'. Allowed: shoes|harness|belay_device|chalk_bag. Pipe-delimited or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Shoe and harness rentals available"
Output: shoes|harness

Content: "We rent shoes, harnesses, belay devices, and chalk bags"
Output: shoes|harness|belay_device|chalk_bag

Content: "No rentals, bring your own gear"
Output: null

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: beginner_friendly
fields.prompt:
Return TRUE if '{{name}}' offers intro classes, beginner programs, or rental equipment; FALSE if advanced-only; 'null' if unclear.

Examples:
Content: "We offer beginner classes and rental equipment"
Output: TRUE

Content: "Great for first-timers, intro classes available" or reviews say "perfect for beginners"
Output: TRUE

Content: "Advanced climbers only, no instruction"
Output: FALSE

Content: "Rental shoes available"
Output: TRUE

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: day_pass_price_local
fields.description: Adult day-pass price in local currency.
fields.prompt:
Find adult day-pass price for '{{name}}'. Look in pricing sections, reviews mentioning "day pass was $XX". Output numeric only (no symbol) or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Day pass: $25"
Output: 25

Content: "Single visit $20" or review says "paid $20 for day pass"
Output: 20

Content: "£15 for a day pass" (UK gym)
Output: 15

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: student_discount
fields.prompt:
Return TRUE if standing student discount exists at '{{name}}'; FALSE if explicitly no discount; 'null' if unclear.

Examples:
Content: "Student discount available" or pricing shows "Student: $15"
Output: TRUE

Content: Reviews mention "they offer student pricing"
Output: TRUE

Content: "No student discounts"
Output: FALSE

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: membership_from_local
fields.prompt:
Return lowest standard adult monthly membership price for '{{name}}'. Look in pricing sections, reviews mentioning "monthly membership $XX". Output numeric only or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Monthly membership starting at $89"
Output: 89

Content: "Basic membership $65/month"
Output: 65

Content: Review says "I pay $75 per month"
Output: 75

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: difficulty_grades
fields.description: Displayed grade span for the section.
fields.prompt:
Return displayed grade range at '{{name}}' (e.g., 'V0–V9' for bouldering or '5.6–5.13b' for ropes). Text or 'null'.

Examples:
Content: "Boulder grades from V0 to V9"
Output: V0-V9

Content: "Routes ranging 5.6 to 5.13b"
Output: 5.6-5.13b

Content: "All difficulty levels"
Output: null

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: working_hour
fields.description: Operating hours in pipe-delimited format by day
fields.prompt:
Extract operating hours for '{{name}}'. Return in EXACT format: 'mon:HH:MM-HH:MM|tue:HH:MM-HH:MM|wed:HH:MM-HH:MM|thu:HH:MM-HH:MM|fri:HH:MM-HH:MM|sat:HH:MM-HH:MM|sun:HH:MM-HH:MM'. Use 24-hour time (00:00-23:59). List each day separately even if hours are the same.

Examples:
Content: "Open Mon-Fri 6am-10pm, Sat-Sun 9am-8pm"
Output: mon:06:00-22:00|tue:06:00-22:00|wed:06:00-22:00|thu:06:00-22:00|fri:06:00-22:00|sat:09:00-20:00|sun:09:00-20:00

Content: "Hours: Monday-Sunday 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM"
Output: mon:07:00-23:00|tue:07:00-23:00|wed:07:00-23:00|thu:07:00-23:00|fri:07:00-23:00|sat:07:00-23:00|sun:07:00-23:00

Now extract from the content for '{{name}}':

fields.name: about
fields.description: Editorial overview of the gym in friendly, local-guide tone
fields.desired_return: 2-3 paragraphs (max 5000 characters) summarizing climbing style, community, features, ideal visitors
fields.prompt:
Write a 3-5 paragraph editorial overview of '{{name}}' at {{address}} in a friendly, local-guide tone. Based ONLY on the provided content, synthesize: climbing style (bouldering/rope/both), community vibe, notable features (facilities, training areas, amenities), and ideal visitors (beginners/advanced/families). Be specific using actual details from the content - do not invent facts. Maximum 5000 characters. If insufficient data to write a meaningful overview, return 'null'.

Example output style:
"Austin Bouldering Project is a premier bouldering-focused climbing gym in the heart of Austin, Texas. Our 15,000 square foot facility features world-class route setting with weekly resets, keeping our problems fresh and challenging for climbers of all levels.

We pride ourselves on fostering an inclusive, welcoming community where everyone from first-timers to seasoned crushers can find their challenge. Our walls range from beginner-friendly slabs to powerful overhangs, with a dedicated training area featuring campus boards, hangboards, and a kilter board.

After your session, relax in our comfortable lounge area or grab a craft coffee from our on-site café. We offer intro classes every weekend, youth programs, and regular community events including comp nights and themed climbing sessions."

Now write for '{{name}}':

fields.name: faq
fields.description: Common visitor questions with factual answers
fields.prompt:
Generate 5-8 realistic Q&A pairs about '{{name}}' based ONLY on factual details in the provided content. Return in JSON format: [{"question": "...", "answer": "..."}, ...]. Focus on practical visitor questions: rentals, first-timer info, age limits, parking, hours, pricing specifics, classes. Only include Q&A you can answer from the content - do not invent information. If insufficient data, return 'null'.

Example:
[
  {"question": "Do I need my own climbing shoes?", "answer": "No! We offer rental shoes and chalk bags at the front desk for $5. However, many climbers prefer to bring their own shoes for a better fit."},
  {"question": "Do you offer intro classes for beginners?", "answer": "Yes! We offer beginner bouldering classes every Saturday at 10am and Sunday at 2pm. Classes are $25 and include gym entry, shoe rental, and 90 minutes of instruction."}
]

Now generate for '{{name}}':

fields.name: why_climbers_like_it
fields.desired_return: JSON array of 3-5 specific positive bullet points: ["point 1", "point 2", ...]
fields.prompt:
Based on reviews and content about '{{name}}', extract 3-5 specific standout positive aspects. Return as JSON array of strings: ["point 1", "point 2", ...]. Be specific and quote-worthy - avoid generic statements. Focus on what makes this gym special.

Examples:
["Fresh route sets every Monday - always new problems to try", "Incredibly friendly community with climbers of all levels", "Spacious layout with minimal crowding even during peak hours", "On-site café serving local coffee and healthy snacks", "Excellent training area with kilter board and spray wall"]

If no review data or positive mentions, return 'null'. Now extract for '{{name}}':

fields.name: rating_route_quality
fields.prompt:
Evaluate route/problem quality at '{{name}}' on a 1.0-5.0 scale. Consider: route creativity, grade consistency, reset frequency, variety of problems/routes mentioned in reviews and content. Be realistic based on evidence.

Rating guide:
- 4.5-5.0: Exceptional (world-class setting, frequent resets, highly praised)
- 4.0-4.4: Very good (creative routes, regular resets, positive feedback)
- 3.5-3.9: Good (decent variety, adequate resets)
- 3.0-3.4: Adequate (basic routes, mixed reviews)
- Below 3.0: Poor (complaints about quality/staleness)

Return float with 1 decimal (e.g., 4.3) or 'null' if insufficient data.

fields.name: rating_cleanliness
fields.prompt: Evaluate facility cleanliness at '{{name}}' on a 1.0-5.0 scale based on reviews mentioning: chalk control, restroom hygiene, air circulation, general tidiness. Be realistic based on evidence. Return float (e.g., 4.5) or 'null' if insufficient data.

fields.name: rating_staff_friendliness
fields.prompt: Evaluate staff friendliness at '{{name}}' on a 1.0-5.0 scale based on reviews mentioning: front desk service, coach helpfulness, staff professionalism, welcoming atmosphere. Be realistic based on evidence. Return float (e.g., 4.9) or 'null' if insufficient data.

fields.name: rating_facilities
fields.prompt: Evaluate facilities quality at '{{name}}' on a 1.0-5.0 scale. Consider: amenities (showers, cafe, lockers), training equipment (hangboards, campus boards), lounge areas, parking mentioned in content and reviews. Be realistic based on evidence. Return float (e.g., 4.3) or 'null' if insufficient data.

fields.name: rating_value_for_money
fields.prompt: Evaluate value-for-money at '{{name}}' on a 1.0-5.0 scale. Consider: pricing relative to gym size, quality, offerings, and amenities based on content and reviews mentioning 'worth it', 'expensive', 'good value', etc. Be realistic based on evidence. Return float (e.g., 4.4) or 'null' if insufficient data.

fields.name: rating_overall
fields.description: Overall rating calculated as average of 5 rating fields
fields.desired_return: Float 1.0-5.0 (average of rating_route_quality, rating_cleanliness, rating_staff_friendliness, rating_facilities, rating_value_for_money)
